title: Codiaeum johannis
intro: Codiaeum johannis, commonly known as the Croton Johanni, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The health benefits of Codiaeum johannis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Codiaeum johannis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Codieum johannis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a member of the genus Codiaeum, which includes about 50 species of plants native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The genus Codiaeum is part of the larger family Euphorbiaceae, which comprises over 2,000 species of flowering plants. Euphorbiaceae is a family of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. These plants are known for their diverse range of shapes, sizes, and growth habits, with many species being valued for their ornamental and medicinal properties.
uses: Codiaeum johannis, commonly known as the Croton plant, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In addition, Codiaeum johannis is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as an anti-diarrheal agent. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.

side_effects: Codiaeum johannis is known to cause skin irritation in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin. Contact with the plant's sap may lead to redness, itching, and minor burns in some cases. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may cause more severe reactions, including blistering and inflammation.
